title: Brand Equity Orientation
description: Build the shared BBE language needed before reading a brand signal or debating action options.
outcome: You can explain why BBE is the core signal system and separate outcome KPIs from input KPIs.
practicePrompt: Given a brand that is still famous but losing energy, name which BBE signals you would inspect first.

title: Read The Signals
audience: Brand managers, insights leads, and strategy partners
description: Learn how to read benchmark, momentum, and price-justification signals without collapsing every warning into the same strategic read.
outcome: You can tell whether a concern is about current strength, peer ambition, trajectory, or a blocked conclusion.
practicePrompt: Classify a signal as category context, Ahead ambition, Momentum trajectory, or Perceived Value guardrail.

title: Interpret The Brand
description: Use typology, life stage, rule logic, and evidence trace language to avoid one-size-fits-all action thinking.
outcome: You can explain why two brands with the same weak KPI may need different action paths.
practicePrompt: Compare a Star and an Outsider with the same weak Different signal and choose what evidence would change the read.

title: Connect BBE To Action
audience: Brand, insights, media, RGM, and shopper partners
description: See how connected systems help explain and act on BBE without replacing the BBE read.
outcome: You can choose the right next evidence lens: Growth Navigator, CEPs, distinctive assets, availability, or evidence readiness.
practicePrompt: Given a BBE read, choose whether the next question is about conversion, retrieval, recognizability, buyability, or confidence.

title: Practice And Certify
audience: Teams preparing to use Brand Doctor in recurring business reviews
description: Add scenario checks, teach-back prompts, and a lightweight grounded-reader certification once the module language is stable.
outcome: Users can demonstrate BBE signal-reading fluency without needing methodology-owner depth.
practicePrompt: Complete a brand case walkthrough, catch the overreach, and explain the recommended next evidence lens.
